title: ActionFailed in Scope
text: When code is ActionFailed and message says no dependent actions succeeded, the failure is usually inside a child action under a Scope/Foreach. Open the run action tree, find the first failed leaf (HTTP, API connection), and read that action outputs.error — that leaf carries the real connector or HTTP error.

title: UnresolvableHostName
text: UnresolvableHostName means the HTTP action URI host could not be resolved by DNS. Fix: correct the hostname in the HTTP action URI, verify private DNS/VNet integration, or use a reachable hostname. Remediation often replaces URI with a known-good endpoint; root cause is DNS/network not request body schema.

title: HTTP 404
text: HTTP 404 means the server could not find the resource for the given path/method. Fix: verify base URL, API version segment, resource id in path, and HTTP method. Compare with API documentation.

title: HTTP 401 and 403
text: 401 Unauthorized: invalid or expired token, wrong audience, or missing Authorization header. 403 Forbidden: token valid but insufficient RBAC or app-level permissions. Fix: refresh connection in Logic Apps, rotate secret, re-consent OAuth, or assign correct Azure RBAC/API permissions.

title: Timeouts and 504
text: Timeouts indicate downstream slowness or blocking calls. Fix: increase HTTP timeout/runtime settings where supported, add bounded retryPolicy on the action, reduce payload size, or call an async pattern if the API supports it.

title: HTTP 400 Bad Request
text: 400 usually means malformed JSON, wrong content-type, missing required fields, or schema validation failure. Fix: compare body against API schema, ensure expressions produce objects not double-encoded strings, and validate required headers.

title: Managed identity auth
text: When using managed identity to call Azure or external APIs, ensure the identity is enabled on the Logic App, the target resource accepts AAD auth, and RBAC roles or app roles grant access. Token audience must match the API.

title: Connector APIConnection
text: Managed connector actions fail with connector-specific codes. Fix: open the connection resource, re-authenticate, verify resource group and API limits, and check connector status in Azure Portal. Use the inner error from outputs for the precise reason.

title: Throttling 429
text: 429 Too Many Requests: implement exponential backoff, reduce concurrency, batch requests, or request quota increase from the service provider.

title: TLS and certificate errors
text: Certificate or TLS errors require fixing server certificate chain, using correct hostname for SAN, or importing trusted roots. For dev only, avoid disabling TLS verification in production.

title: Invalid workflow expression
text: Expression evaluation errors reference the function or token that failed. Fix the workflow definition expression syntax or ensure referenced outputs exist from prior actions.

title: Action retryPolicy
text: For transient failures, add action-level retryPolicy (type fixed or exponential) with sensible count and interval. Combine with idempotent APIs where possible.
